definition: The legal process of transferring ownership of property from the seller to the buyer. It involves searches, enquiries, exchanging contracts and completing the sale.
A tax charged by the UK government when you buy property or land over a certain price in England and Northern Ireland. The amount depends on the purchase price and whether you are a first-time buyer.
The point at which the sale becomes legally binding. Both buyer and seller sign contracts and the buyer pays their deposit. Neither party can withdraw without financial penalty after exchange.
The day ownership of the property formally transfers to the buyer. Keys are handed over and the purchase price is paid in full. The buyer can move in on completion day.
The ratio of your mortgage to the value of the property, expressed as a percentage. A £180,000 mortgage on a £200,000 property is 90% LTV. Lower LTV usually means better mortgage rates.
A form of property ownership where you own the property for a fixed number of years but not the land it stands on. You pay ground rent and service charge to the freeholder.
Outright ownership of a property and the land it sits on with no time limit. Most houses are freehold. Freehold owners have no ground rent or service charge to pay.
A document rating a property's energy efficiency from A (most efficient) to G (least efficient). Sellers must provide an EPC before marketing the property. It also recommends improvements.
